blocks.text: institution but in no case shall exceed 24 units per semester or term (CMO 40, s.2008; CM0 30, s.2009).
Section 5 Any teaching load in excess of the normal or regular

teaching load shall be considered as overload. Overload partakes of the nature of temporary extra assignment and compensation therefore shall be considered as an overload honorarium if performed within the 8-hour work period and does not form part of the regular or basic pay.
Overload performed beyond the eight-hour daily work is
overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).
Section 6 Honoraria may be paid to teaching personnel engaged in
actual classroom teaching, whose teaching load is outside of the regular office hours or in excess of the regular load
(Sec.63 (a) General Provisions of RA 11975).
Section 7 Government employees whose regular schedule of work
falls between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m., including those under the flexible work arrangements whose nature of services requires their rendition within the same period are
excluded from the grant of Night Shift Differential Pay. The
services they rendered beyond the regular eight (8) – hour work schedules shall be compensated in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations on overtime
services and overtime pay (CSC-DBM-GCGOCCs Joint Circular No. 1, s.2023).
7.1 An employee whose prescribed work of schedule is from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. but renders services until 7 p.m.
as authorized by the head of agency. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift
Differential Pay, but shall be compensated in accordance
with the rules and regulations an overtime services and overtime pay.

7.2 An employee whose agency implements compressed workweek with ten (10) working hours for four days in a week, has the prescribed work schedule from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. with 12 p.m. to 1 p.m. as the time for lunch.
The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be
covered by Night Shift Differential Pay. In case the

employee renders services beyond the prescribed ten (10) hours of work, as authorized by the head of agency, he/she shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

fixed_blocks.text:
2 institution but in no case shall exceed 24 units per semester or term (CMO 40, s.2008; CM0 30, s.2009).

Section 5 Any teaching load in excess of the normal or regular teaching load shall be considered as overload. Overload partakes of the nature of temporary extra assignment and compensation therefore shall be considered as an overload honorarium if within the 8-hour work period and does not form part of the regular or basic pay.

Overload performed beyond the eight-hour daily work is overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).

Section 6 Honoraria may be paid to teaching personnel engaged in actual classroom teaching, whose teaching load is outside of the regular office hours or in excess of the regular load (Sec.63 (a) General Provisions of RA 11975).

Section 7 Government employees whose regular schedule of work falls between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m., including those under the flexible work arrangements whose nature of services requires their rendition within the same period are excluded from the grant of Night Shift Differential Pay. The services they rendered beyond the regular eight (8)-hour work schedules shall be compensated in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay (CSC-DBM-GCGOCCs Joint Circular No. 1, s.2023).

7.1 An employee whose prescribed work schedule is from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., but renders services until 7 p.m., as authorized by the head of agency. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay, but shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

7.2 An employee whose agency implements compressed workweek with ten (10) working hours for four days in a week, has the prescribed work schedule from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m., with 12 p.m. to 1 p.m. as the time for lunch. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay. In case the employee renders services beyond the prescribed ten (10) hours of work, as authorized by the head of agency, he/she shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

qa_pairs.context:
2 institution but in no case shall exceed 24 units per semester or term (CMO 40, s.2008; CM0 30, s.2009).

Section 5 Any teaching load in excess of the normal or regular teaching load shall be considered as overload. Overload partakes of the nature of temporary extra assignment and compensation therefore shall be considered as an overload honorarium if within the 8-hour work period and does not form part of the regular or basic pay.

Overload performed beyond the eight-hour daily work is overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).

Section 6 Honoraria may be paid to teaching personnel engaged in actual classroom teaching, whose teaching load is outside of the regular office hours or in excess of the regular load (Sec.63 (a) General Provisions of RA 11975).

Section 7 Government employees whose regular schedule of work falls between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m., including those under the flexible work arrangements whose nature of services requires their rendition within the same period are excluded from the grant of Night Shift Differential Pay. The services they rendered beyond the regular eight (8)-hour work schedules shall be compensated in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay (CSC-DBM-GCGOCCs Joint Circular No. 1, s.2023).

7.1 An employee whose prescribed work schedule is from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., but renders services until 7 p.m., as authorized by the head of agency. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay, but shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

7.2 An employee whose agency implements compressed workweek with ten (10) working hours for four days in a week, has the prescribed work schedule from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m., with 12 p.m. to 1 p.m. as the time for lunch. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay. In case the employee renders services beyond the prescribed ten (10) hours of work, as authorized by the head of agency, he/she shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

Section 7 Government employees whose regular schedule of work falls between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m., including those under the flexible work arrangements whose nature of services requires their rendition within the same period are excluded from the grant of Night Shift Differential Pay. The services they rendered beyond the regular eight (8)-hour work schedules shall be compensated in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay (CSC-DBM-GCGOCCs Joint Circular No. 1, s.2023).

7.1 An employee whose prescribed work schedule is from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., but renders services until 7 p.m., as authorized by the head of agency. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay, but shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

7.2 An employee whose agency implements compressed workweek with ten (10) working hours for four days in a week, has the prescribed work schedule from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m., with 12 p.m. to 1 p.m. as the time for lunch. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay. In case the employee renders services beyond the prescribed ten (10) hours of work, as authorized by the head of agency, he/she shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.
qa_pairs.question: Can teaching load exceed the normal or regular teaching load?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it can be considered as overload.

qa_pairs.answer: Any teaching load beyond the eight-hour daily work period.

qa_pairs.answer: To compensate government employees for rendering services beyond the regular eight-hour work schedules.

Section 7 Government employees whose regular schedule of work falls between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m., including those under the flexible work arrangements whose nature of services requires their rendition within the same period are excluded from the grant of Night Shift Differential Pay. The services they rendered beyond the regular eight (8)-hour work schedules shall be compensated in accordance with the existing laws, rules, and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay (CSC-DBM-GCGOCCs Joint Circular No. 1, s.2023).

7.1 An employee whose prescribed work schedule is from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., but renders services until 7 p.m., as authorized by the head of agency. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay, but shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.

7.2 An employee whose agency implements compressed workweek with ten (10) working hours for four days in a week, has the prescribed work schedule from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m., with 12 p.m. to 1 p.m. as the time for lunch. The hours worked from 6 p.m. to 7 p.m. shall not be covered by Night Shift Differential Pay. In case the employee renders services beyond the prescribed ten (10) hours of work, as authorized by the head of agency, he/she shall be compensated in accordance with the rules and regulations on overtime services and overtime pay.
qa_pairs.question: Are there any exceptions to the rule on Night Shift Differential Pay?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, government employees whose regular schedule of work falls between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m. are excluded from the grant of Night Shift Differential Pay.

Section 5 Any teaching load in excess of the normal or regular teaching load shall be considered as overload. Overload partakes of the nature of temporary extra assignment and compensation therefore shall be considered as an overload honorarium if within the 8-hour work period and does not form part of the regular or basic pay.

Overload performed beyond the eight-hour daily work is overtime work (CMO 40, s.2008; CMO 30, s.2009).

qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if authorized by the head of agency.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of CMO 40 and CMO 30?
qa_pairs.answer: To regulate the teaching load and overload in government schools and institutions.
